methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $350M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Fairmat is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Benjamin Saada, currently valued at $350M post-money following €95M (~$103M) in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies: 1 public listing (277810.KQ) and 8 private robotics firms at equivalent stage, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and GrayMatter Robotics.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B to next-stage advancement, yielding $665M. Peer-median methodology benchmarks against five same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.6B, though the peer set exhibits material dispersion ranging from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). The peer median reflects mid-market positioning within this wide distribution.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $565M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a midpoint of $1.2B. This implies 61% upside to the current $350M valuation at the median case. Key uncertainties include the extreme outlier valuation of 1X Technologies at $10.0B, which materially influences peer comparability, and the absence of comparable public market data sufficient for revenue multiple benchmarking. The wide peer range ($300M–$10B) reflects sector immaturity and heterogeneous company profiles. Capital efficiency and path to Series C financing represent primary valuation drivers within this range.
